title: 1.1: Tests that DKIM validation returns three failure indicators when no DNS CNAME records are configured
1.2: Tests that DKIM validation sets the domain verification flag to false when records are missing
1.3: Tests that DKIM validation returns failure indicators only for incorrectly configured records when some records have wrong values
1.4: Tests that DKIM validation preserves verified status for domains using legacy single-record system
1.5: Tests that DKIM validation returns indicators for missing additional records in legacy-verified domains
1.6: Tests that DKIM validation returns empty result when all three DKIM CNAME records are set to expected values
1.7: Tests that DKIM validation sets the domain verification flag to true when all records are valid
1.8: Tests that wrong values scenario assertions do not check for 'empty' indicators
2.1: Places the test for unconfigured DKIM records in tests/ directory
2.2: Places the test for wrong record values in tests/ directory
2.3: Places the test for legacy single-record system in tests/ directory
2.4: Places the test for successful DKIM configuration in tests/ directory
3.1: All tests use the pytest framework
3.2: All tests follow the test_ prefix naming pattern
3.3: All tests use standard assert statements for verification
4.1: Follows DNS mocking pattern using InMemoryDNSClient for all DKIM validation tests
4.2: Follows database verification pattern by checking dkim_verified flag after validation
